# `osc` sequences

<!-- Generated by `qdb generate reference` from `db/osc.toml`. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Set Icon And Window Title (`osc.0.title_icon`)

OSC may be BEL-terminated or ST-terminated.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 0 ; text ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/title_icon.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/title_icon.seq)

## Set Icon Title (`osc.1.icon_title`)

Sets only the icon title where supported.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1 ; text ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/title_icon_only.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/title_icon_only.seq)

## Set Window Title (`osc.2.window_title`)

Sets only the window title where supported.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 2 ; text ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/title_window.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/title_window.seq)

## OSC 8 Hyperlink (`osc.8.hyperlink`)

Close with an empty OSC 8 URI.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 8 ; params ; uri ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/hyperlink.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/hyperlink.seq)

## OSC 8 Hyperlink Close (`osc.8.hyperlink.close`)

Closes the currently open hyperlink so following text is not linked.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 8 ; ; ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/hyperlink_close.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/hyperlink_close.seq)

## Query Foreground Color (`osc.10.foreground_query`)

Asks the terminal to report its current default foreground color.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 10 ; ? ST`
- Responds with: `osc.10.foreground_report`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/foreground_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/foreground_query.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty supported (25); betamax no-reply; foot supported (25); kitty supported (25); tmux no-reply; wezterm supported (25); xterm supported (25)

## Foreground Color Report (`osc.10.foreground_report`)

Reports the terminal's current default foreground color.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- Syntax: `OSC 10 ; color ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_kitty.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_kitty.seq), [`fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_alacritty.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_alacritty.seq), [`fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_wezterm.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_wezterm.seq), [`fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_foot.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_foot.seq), [`fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_xterm.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/osc_10_foreground_report_report_capture_xterm.seq)
- Notes: Syntax from live captures; `color` is xterm's typed `rgb:rrrr/gggg/bbbb` form on every answering target.

## Set Foreground Color (`osc.10.foreground_set`)

Fixture uses typed xterm `rgb:rrrr/gggg/bbbb` syntax.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 10 ; color ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/foreground_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/foreground_set.seq)

## Set Foreground Color With Hex RGB (`osc.10.foreground_set_hex`)

Fixture exercises typed `#rrggbb` validation before OSC encoding.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 10 ; #rrggbb ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06); [WezTerm Escape Sequences](https://wezterm.org/escape-sequences.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/foreground_set_hex.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/foreground_set_hex.seq)

- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/background_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/background_reset.seq)

## Reset Cursor Color (`osc.112.cursor_reset`)

Resets the text-cursor color to its configured value.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 112 ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/cursor_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/cursor_reset.seq)

## OSC 52 Clipboard Write (`osc.52.clipboard`)

Clipboard reads and writes require explicit policy.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 52 ; target ; payload ST`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/clipboard.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/clipboard.seq), [`fixtures/osc/negative/policy_denied/clipboard.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/negative/policy_denied/clipboard.seq)

## OSC 52 Clipboard Query (`osc.52.clipboard_query`)

Fixture queries target `c`; terminals may deny or ignore clipboard reads.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 52 ; target ; ? ST`
- Responds with: `osc.52.clipboard_report`
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/clipboard_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/clipboard_query.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty no-reply; betamax no-reply; foot no-reply; kitty no-reply; tmux no-reply; wezterm no-reply; xterm no-reply

## OSC 52 Clipboard Report (`osc.52.clipboard_report`)

Payload is base64 text; decoding belongs in a higher helper.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [XTerm Control Sequences (invisible-island)](https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## Legacy Notification (`osc.9.notification`)

Legacy notification convention implemented by some terminals.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 9 ; message ST`
- References: [kitty Desktop Notifications (source documentation)](https://github.com/kovidgoyal/kitty/blob/master/docs/desktop-notifications.rst) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/notification_legacy.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/notification_legacy.seq)

## Notify Notification (`osc.777.notification`)

Common notify convention with typed title/body metadata.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 777 ; notify ; title ; body ST`
- References: [kitty Desktop Notifications (source documentation)](https://github.com/kovidgoyal/kitty/blob/master/docs/desktop-notifications.rst) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/notification_notify.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/notification_notify.seq), [`fixtures/osc/negative/policy_denied/notification_notify.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/negative/policy_denied/notification_notify.seq)

## Kitty Desktop Notification (`kitty.notification.osc99`)

Fixture uses a single metadata/payload chunk; OSC 99 supports richer chunking.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 99 ; metadata ; payload ST`
- References: [kitty Desktop Notifications Protocol](https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/desktop-notifications/) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/notification_kitty.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/notification_kitty.seq)

## Current Working Directory (`osc.7.cwd`)

Typed helpers validate the host and absolute path before encoding a file URI.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 7 ; uri ST`
- References: [terminal-wg specifications, issue 20](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/terminal-wg/specifications/-/issues/20) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_cwd.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_cwd.seq)

## Prompt Start (`finalterm.shell.prompt_start`)

Marks the start of a shell prompt.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 133 ; A ST`
- References: [terminal-wg specifications, issue 14](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/terminal-wg/specifications/-/issues/14) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_prompt_start.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_prompt_start.seq)

## Prompt End (`finalterm.shell.prompt_end`)

Marks the end of a shell prompt.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 133 ; B ST`
- References: [terminal-wg specifications, issue 14](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/terminal-wg/specifications/-/issues/14) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_prompt_end.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_prompt_end.seq)

## Command Start (`finalterm.shell.command_start`)

Marks the beginning of command output.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 133 ; C ST`
- References: [terminal-wg specifications, issue 14](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/terminal-wg/specifications/-/issues/14) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_command_start.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_command_start.seq)

## Command Finished (`finalterm.shell.command_finished`)

Fixture includes exit code 1.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 133 ; D [ ; exit-code ] ST`
- References: [terminal-wg specifications, issue 14](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/terminal-wg/specifications/-/issues/14) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_command_finished.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_command_finished.seq)

## VS Code Prompt Start (`vscode.shell.prompt_start`)

VS Code recommends writing OSC 633 sequences only when `TERM_PROGRAM=vscode`.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; A ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_prompt_start.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_prompt_start.seq)

## VS Code Prompt End (`vscode.shell.prompt_end`)

Rich command detection expects prompt start before prompt end.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; B ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_prompt_end.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_prompt_end.seq)

## VS Code Command Executed (`vscode.shell.command_executed`)

VS Code describes this as the pre-execution marker.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; C ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_command_executed.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_command_executed.seq)

## VS Code Command Finished (`vscode.shell.command_finished`)

Fixture includes exit code 0; the exit code is optional.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; D [ ; exitcode ] ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_command_finished.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_command_finished.seq)

## VS Code Command Line (`vscode.shell.command_line`)

Command payload escapes semicolon and characters `0x20` and below with `\\xAB`.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; E ; commandline ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_command_line.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_command_line.seq)

## VS Code Command Line With Nonce (`vscode.shell.command_line_nonce`)

Nonce allows VS Code to verify that the sequence came from shell integration.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; E ; commandline ; nonce ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_command_line_nonce.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_command_line_nonce.seq)

## VS Code Shell Property (`vscode.shell.property`)

Only properties known to VS Code are handled.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; P ; name=value ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_vscode_property.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_vscode_property.seq)

## VS Code Current Working Directory (`vscode.shell.cwd`)

Reports the shell current working directory for link resolution and tab state.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; P ; Cwd=path ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_cwd.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_cwd.seq)

## VS Code Windows Backend Property (`vscode.shell.is_windows`)

Valid values are `True` and `False`; VS Code uses this for Windows backend heuristics.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; P ; IsWindows=True|False ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_is_windows.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_is_windows.seq)

## VS Code Rich Command Detection Property (`vscode.shell.rich_command_detection`)

Set to `True` when markers are emitted in VS Code's expected `A, B, E, C, D` order.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 633 ; P ; HasRichCommandDetection=True|False ST`
- References: [Visual Studio Code: Terminal Shell Integration escape sequences](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/shell-integration#_supported-escape-sequences) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/vscode_rich_command_detection.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/vscode_rich_command_detection.seq)

## iTerm2 Current Directory (`iterm2.osc1337.current_dir`)

iTerm2 convention, preserved here as a typed shell-integration helper.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; CurrentDir=path ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_iterm_cwd.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_iterm_cwd.seq)

## iTerm2 Set Mark (`iterm2.osc1337.set_mark`)

Marks the current prompt/output location in iTerm2-compatible terminals.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; SetMark ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/shell_iterm_mark.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/shell_iterm_mark.seq)

## iTerm2 File Transfer (`iterm2.osc1337.file`)

The `name` argument is base64-encoded per the iTerm2 inline images protocol.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; File=args:base64-payload ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06); [iTerm2 Inline Images Protocol](https://iterm2.com/documentation-images.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/iterm2_file.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/iterm2_file.seq)
- Notes: MultipartFile/FilePart/FileEnd remains preserve-only.

## iTerm2 Inline Image (`iterm2.osc1337.inline_image`)

Inline images share the OSC 1337 File command with `inline=1` metadata.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; File=inline=1;args:base64-payload ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06); [iTerm2 Inline Images Protocol](https://iterm2.com/documentation-images.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/osc/iterm2_inline_image.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/iterm2_inline_image.seq), [`fixtures/osc/iterm2_inline_image_sized.seq`](../../../fixtures/osc/iterm2_inline_image_sized.seq)
- Notes: MultipartFile/FilePart/FileEnd remains preserve-only.
